strategy_summary: The binary is vulnerable to a format string attack. We can use the printf function to overwrite the GOT entry of a function (e.g., puts) with the address of main, which will cause an infinite loop. This will allow us to bypass any checks and potentially leak libc addresses. Since the binary has partial RELRO, we need to find a way to get a one-gadget ROP chain from libc. We will first measure the offset to control the stack and then find the exact offset to overwrite the puts GOT entry.
